answer:
Thinking about the most important things I've learned from my parents, there are definitely some good lessons and, unfortunately, a few not-so-good ones too. I'll start with the positive. 

The best thing my parents taught me was the value of hard work. They both came from pretty humble beginnings and worked incredibly hard to provide a good life for me and my siblings. I remember my dad working long hours at his construction job, coming home exhausted but still making time for family. And my mom built a career while also taking care of all of us kids. Their work ethic showed me that determination and perseverance pay off.

Another great lesson from my parents was to be independent and take care of myself. They could've coddled me growing up, but instead, they gave me responsibility at a young age. I had chores and a part-time job in high school, and I paid for a lot of my own expenses. This taught me to rely on myself and do what needs to be done without waiting for help. Their trust in me boosted my confidence.

One not-so-great thing I observed was my parents' short tempers. They could both fly off the handle pretty quickly over little things. Seeing their angry reactions definitely influenced me to also be quick-tempered in my youth. It took maturing and actively working on patience to avoid following that path. I wish they had handled stress better when I was young.

The other negative lesson was their tendency to be overcritical. They had impossibly high standards and unrealistic expectations that made me feel inadequate at times. To this day, I sometimes doubt myself or struggle with perfectionism. I'm trying to be more self-compassionate, but their criticism still echoes in my mind. 

So, in short - hard work, independence, and short fuses were the biggest takeaways from Mom and Dad. I'm thankful for the positive lessons that shaped my values. But seeing their flaws helps me identify things I want to improve on myself as a person.
title: What is the most important thing you have learned from your parents, both bad and good? Explain your reasoning.
